post.description:
Authenticate a user and return a JWT access and refresh token pair.

- **email**: Registered email address
- **password**: User password
- **mfa_code**: Required if Multi-Factor Authentication is enabled for the account
Invalidate the current session's tokens and log the user out.

Create a new user account.

- Validates email uniqueness
- Enforces strong password requirements
- Triggers an asynchronous email verification flow
- Supports idempotency via **Idempotency-Key** header
Complete the registration process by verifying the user's email address using a token.

Rotate the current refresh token to obtain a fresh access token.
The provided refresh token will be invalidated upon use.

Initiate the password recovery process.

Always returns a success message even if the email is not registered to prevent account enumeration.
Finalize password recovery using the token received via email.
Update the password for the currently logged-in user.

Begin Multi-Factor Authentication setup. 
Returns a secret and a QR code URI for use with authenticator apps.
Verify the MFA code to complete activation of Multi-Factor Authentication.

Deactivate Multi-Factor Authentication. 
Requires a valid MFA code for confirmation.

get.description: Retrieve the profile information of the currently authenticated user.

delete.description:
Deactivate the current user's account.

This performs a **soft delete**, marking the account as inactive. 
Authenticated requests will no longer be possible once completed.

patch.description:
Update the profile details of the currently authenticated user.

Updating the **email** will require re-verification.

get.description: Get detailed usage statistics and remaining rate limits for the current user.

Retrieve user information by user ID. 

**Restricted to Enterprise/Admin users only.**

List all users with pagination and filtering.

**Restricted to Enterprise/Admin users only.**

post.description:
Predict option price using machine learning models.
Proxies request to the specialized ML serving microservice.

Calculate the theoretical price of a single option.

Supports multiple models:
- **black_scholes**: Standard analytical model
- **monte_carlo**: Stochastic simulation
- **binomial**: Cox-Ross-Rubinstein lattice model

Calculate prices for multiple options in a single batch request.

Useful for portfolio-wide valuations. Maximum 1000 options per request.
Individual failures in the batch are skipped and logged.

Calculate all standard option Greeks (Delta, Gamma, Theta, Vega, Rho).

Currently uses the Black-Scholes analytical model for Greeks.

Calculate the implied volatility from a given market price.

Uses the Newton-Raphson numerical method.

Price exotic options:
- **asian**: Geometric (analytical) or Arithmetic (Monte Carlo)
- **barrier**: Knock-in/out (analytical)
- **lookback**: Floating/Fixed strike (analytical/MC)
- **digital**: Cash-or-nothing (analytical)

get.description: Detailed health check including database and cache status.
